The Zen Qing clan traces its origins back to imperial China when it was believed that the realms of heaven and earth were closely connected. Emperor Huang Zen, a wise and pious ruler, often sought divine guidance for his people. Emperor Huang Zen met a celestial maiden who was told to carry the blood of golden dragons who were mythical guardians of heaven.

The emperor and Yule woman fell in love. Their union produced a son, Zenlong, the first dragon-born human, with the blood of dragons in his veins. His birth marked the beginning of the Zen Qing clan, whose members were seen as divine, blessed by the gods and the dragons.

When Zenlong became emperor, the Zen Qing dynasty led China into a period of prosperity, excelling in both military and cultural achievements. The dynasty maintained a close bond with the celestial dragons, who were said to protect the empire as long as their descendants ruled. Dragon-born rulers were believed to have special powers, and their authority was regarded as absolute. The purity of their bloodline was carefully maintained through selective marriages, ensuring that their dragon heritage remained strong.
